evals.name: errands-task-obvious-label
evals.prompt:
Enrich this task: **Pick up dry cleaning**
Description: From the place on High Street. Closes at 6pm.
Project: One-Off Tasks
evals.expected_output: Should apply @errands label silently (without asking) and propose a due date or note the 6pm constraint. Should not invent labels.

assertions.name: applies-errands-label
assertions.criteria: The response applies or proposes an errands-related context label (e.g. @errands) without asking the user whether to add it.

assertions.name: does-not-ask-about-label
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT ask the user 'should I add an @errands label?' — it applies it confidently or includes it in a batch proposal.

assertions.name: proposes-batch
assertions.criteria: The response presents all proposed metadata changes together in one summary, not one at a time.

evals.name: waiting-for-detection
evals.prompt:
Enrich this task: **Chase landlord about broken boiler**
Description: Reported it two weeks ago, no response yet. Need to follow up.
Project: One-Off Tasks
evals.expected_output: Should detect this as a waiting-for situation and apply @waiting label with a note about what it's waiting on.

assertions.name: detects-waiting-for
assertions.criteria: The response identifies this as a waiting-for situation — the user is waiting on the landlord's response.

assertions.name: applies-waiting-label
assertions.criteria: The response proposes or applies a @waiting label (or similar waiting-for label).

assertions.name: notes-what-waiting-on
assertions.criteria: The response notes or proposes noting what the task is waiting on (landlord's response about the boiler).

evals.name: task-with-hard-deadline
evals.prompt:
Enrich this task: **Submit tax return**
Description: Self-assessment for 2024-25. Deadline is January 31st. Accountant has the figures, just need to file online.
Project: One-Off Tasks
evals.expected_output: Should distinguish between due date (when to work on it) and deadline (January 31st). Should set appropriate priority given the hard deadline.

assertions.name: identifies-deadline
assertions.criteria: The response identifies January 31st as a hard deadline or external due date.

assertions.name: sets-priority
assertions.criteria: The response proposes a priority level for this task.

assertions.name: proposes-batch
assertions.criteria: The response presents all proposed metadata changes together in one summary.

evals.name: someday-maybe-task
evals.prompt:
Enrich this task: **Learn to play guitar**
Description: Always wanted to try. No rush.
Project: Someday / Maybe
evals.expected_output: Should skip priority and due date for someday/maybe items. Should ensure the description is solid for future review.

assertions.name: skips-priority
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose a priority for this task, or explicitly states that priority doesn't apply to someday/maybe items.

assertions.name: skips-due-date
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT propose a due date for this task, or explicitly states that due dates don't apply to someday/maybe items.

assertions.name: acknowledges-someday-maybe
assertions.criteria: The response recognises this is a someday/maybe item and treats it differently from an active task.

evals.name: already-enriched-task
evals.prompt:
Enrich this task: **Call accountant about tax return**
Description: Tax return is overdue, risk of fine. Accountant is James at Smith & Co.
Project: One-Off Tasks
Priority: p2
Labels: @phone
Due date: Tomorrow
evals.expected_output: Should recognise the task already has appropriate metadata and not propose unnecessary changes.

assertions.name: recognises-already-enriched
assertions.criteria: The response recognises that the task already has appropriate metadata (priority, label, due date) and does not propose redundant changes.

assertions.name: does-not-over-enrich
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT add unnecessary extra labels or change metadata that is already appropriate.
